2605.05748.title: Evaluating Explainability in Safety-Critical ATR Systems: Limitations of Post-Hoc Methods and Paths Toward Robust XAI
2605.05748.paper_summary: Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) is increasingly rec ognized as essential for deploying machine learning systems in safety critical environments. In Automatic Target Recognition (ATR), where models operate on image, video, radar, and multisensor data, high pre dictive performance alone is insufficient. Model decisions must also be interpretable, reliable, and suitable for validation. This paper presents a structured evaluation of explainability methods in the context of safety-critical ATR systems: We identify major XAI paradigms, including saliency-based, attention-based, and surrogate ap proaches, as well as recent detection-aware extensions. Based on this, we formalize explainability as an assurance-oriented assessment problem, introduce a taxonomy, and assess these methods with respect to four key dimensions: interpretability, robustness, vulnerability to manipula tion, and suitability for validation and verification. The analysis identifies systematic limitations of current post-hoc explanation methods. In par ticular, we derive critical failure modes such as spurious explanations, instability under perturbations, and overtrust induced by visually con vincing outputs. These findings indicate that widely used XAI techniques may be insufficient for safety-critical deployment. Finally, we discuss implications for ATR systems and outline directions toward more robust, causally grounded, and physically informed explain ability methods. Our results emphasize the need to move beyond visually plausible explanations toward approaches that support reliable decision making and system-level assurance.
2605.05748.paper_summary_zh: 可解釋的人工智慧 (XAI) 正日益被認為對於在安全關鍵環境中部署機器學習系統至關重要。在自動目標識別 (ATR) 中，模型在圖像、視頻、雷達和多傳感器數據上運作，僅有高預測性能是不夠的。模型的決策必須是可解釋的、可靠的，並且適合驗證。本文在安全關鍵的 ATR 系統背景下，對可解釋性方法進行了結構化評估：我們確定了主要的 XAI 範式，包括基於顯著性的、基於注意力的和代理方法，以及最近的檢測感知擴展。在此基礎上，我們將可解釋性形式化為一個以保證為導向的評估問題，介紹了一個分類法，並根據四個關鍵維度評估這些方法：可解釋性、穩健性、對操控的脆弱性，以及適合驗證和確認。分析揭示了當前事後解釋方法的系統性局限性。特別是，我們推導出關鍵失效模式，如虛假解釋、在擾動下的不穩定性，以及由視覺上令人信服的輸出引起的過度信任。這些發現表明，廣泛使用的 XAI 技術可能不足以支持安全關鍵的部署。最後，我們討論了對 ATR 系統的影響，並概述了朝著更穩健、因果基礎和物理知識驅動的可解釋性方法的方向。我們的結果強調需要超越視覺上合理的解釋，朝向支持可靠決策和系統級保證的方法。
2605.05748.author: Vanessa Buhrmester et.al.
2605.05748.authors: Vanessa Buhrmester, David Muench, Dimitri Bulatov, Michael Arens
